dashboard.welcome_text: You haven't completed any assessments yet. Let's start with your baby's first clinical milestone check.

vaccines.title: Vaccinations
vaccines.subtitle: National Immunization Schedule for {{name}}
vaccines.permission_required: Notification permissions are required for reminders.

profile.name: Name
profile.backup_note: Grow Baby Grow is offline-first. Your data never leaves this device unless you export a backup.

v_6w_dpt1.name: DPT-1
v_6w_dpt1.description: Diphtheria, Pertussis, and Tetanus vaccine dose 1

v_10w_dpt2.name: DPT-2
v_10w_dpt2.description: Diphtheria, Pertussis, and Tetanus vaccine dose 2

v_14w_dpt3.name: DPT-3
v_14w_dpt3.description: Diphtheria, Pertussis, and Tetanus vaccine dose 3

v_16m_opvb.name: OPV Booster
v_16m_opvb.description: Booster dose for Oral Polio Vaccine

v_18m_varicella2.name: Varicella (Chicken Pox) - 2nd Dose
v_18m_varicella2.description: Second dose of Chicken Pox (Varicella) vaccine

gm_000_1.milestone: Complete head lag on pull to sit; pelvis high with knees under abdomen
gm_000_1.laymanDescription: The baby's head drops back when gently pulled up to sit.
gm_000_1.howToTest: Gently hold your baby's hands and slowy pull them towards a sitting position. Their head will naturally lag behind.

gm_002_1.milestone: Less head lag on pull to sit; maintains head in plane with body on ventral suspension; head in midline in prone
gm_002_1.laymanDescription: The baby can keep their head more level with their body now.
gm_002_1.howToTest: When pulled to sit, the baby now tries to keep their head in line with their chest rather than letting it flop back completely.

gm_003_1.milestone: Bears weight on forearm; face-couch angle 45-90 degrees; only slight head lag on pull to sit
gm_003_1.laymanDescription: When lying on their tummy, the baby can lift their head and chest using their arms.
gm_003_1.howToTest: Place your baby on their tummy. See if they can look up and support themselves on their forearms.

gm_005_1.laymanDescription: The baby can keep their head steady when pulled to a sitting position.
gm_005_1.howToTest: When you pull your baby by the hands into a sitting position, their head should stay in line with their body without falling back.

gm_006_1.milestone: Keeps chest and upper abdomen off couch; rolls prone to supine; sits with back support
gm_006_1.laymanDescription: The baby can roll over from tummy to back and sit with some support.
gm_006_1.howToTest: While on their tummy, see if your baby can push up and roll onto their back. Also, observe if they can sit up when propped with pillows.

gm_007_1.milestone: Sits on couch with hand support; weight on one hand; rolls supine to prone
gm_007_1.laymanDescription: The baby can sit by leaning on their hands and roll from back to tummy.
gm_007_1.howToTest: See if your baby can sit for a few moments by using their arms for balance. Check if they can roll from their back onto their tummy.

gm_009_1.laymanDescription: The baby starts to crawl and can pull themselves up to stand by holding onto things.
gm_009_1.howToTest: Place a toy slightly out of reach to encourage crawling. Observe if they use furniture to pull themselves up to a standing position.
gm_009_1.redFlagMessage: No sitting without support at 9 months

gm_010_1.milestone: Creeps on hands and knees with abdomen off
gm_010_1.laymanDescription: The baby moves on hands and knees with their belly off the floor (scooting).
gm_010_1.howToTest: Observe if the baby can move across the floor on all fours without dragging their tummy.

gm_011_1.milestone: Cruises (walks holding furniture); pivots to pick object
gm_011_1.laymanDescription: The baby 'cruises' along furniture by holding on and walking sideways.
gm_011_1.howToTest: While standing at a sofa or coffee table, see if your baby can take steps sideways while holding on for support.

gm_012_1.laymanDescription: The baby take steps when you hold just one of their hands.
gm_012_1.howToTest: Hold your baby's hand gently and encourage them to walk. See if they can balance and take steps with minimal support.
gm_012_1.redFlagMessage: No standing with assistance at 12 months

gm_015_1.laymanDescription: The baby can walk a few steps without any help or holding on.
gm_015_1.howToTest: Encourage your baby to walk from one person to another or towards a favorite toy without holding your hand.
gm_015_1.redFlagMessage: Unable to stand alone at 17 months

gm_018_1.laymanDescription: The baby starts to run, though it might look a bit awkward or stiff.
gm_018_1.howToTest: Observe your baby moving quickly across a safe area. They should be able to move faster than a walk, even if their gait is still developing.
gm_018_1.redFlagMessage: Unable to walk alone at 18 months

gm_024_1.milestone: Goes up/down stairs two feet per step; runs properly
gm_024_1.laymanDescription: The baby can walk up and down stairs placing both feet on each step.
gm_024_1.howToTest: At a staircase with a railing or your hand for safety, watch if the baby can climb up or down by putting both feet on one step before moving to the next.
gm_024_1.redFlagMessage: Unable to walk upstairs with help at 24 months

gm_030_1.milestone: Jumps with both feet; walks on tiptoe
gm_030_1.laymanDescription: The baby can jump off the ground with both feet at once.
gm_030_1.howToTest: See if your baby can jump up from the floor or jump down from a very low step using both feet simultaneously.

gm_036_1.milestone: Rides tricycle; up stairs one foot per step; downstairs two feet per step
gm_036_1.laymanDescription: The child can ride a three-wheeled bike and use alternating feet on stairs.
gm_036_1.howToTest: Observe if your child can pedal a tricycle and if they can walk upstairs using one foot for each step (alternating).

gm_048_1.milestone: Downstairs one foot per step; skips on one foot
gm_048_1.laymanDescription: The child can walk downstairs using alternating feet and hop on one foot.
gm_048_1.howToTest: Watch as your child goes downstairs—they should now use one foot per step. Also, see if they can balance and hop on one leg.

gm_060_1.laymanDescription: The child can skip using alternating feet with good coordination.
gm_060_1.howToTest: See if your child can perform a skipping motion (step-hop) while moving forward.

fm_001_1.laymanDescription: The baby automatically curls their fingers around an object placed in their palm.
fm_001_1.howToTest: Place your finger or a slim toy in your baby's palm and see if they grip it tightly.

fm_003_1.milestone: No grasp reflex; retains rattle if placed in hand
fm_003_1.laymanDescription: The baby can hold onto a rattle if you put it in their hand.
fm_003_1.howToTest: Place a light rattle in your baby's hand. They should be able to hold it for a few moments rather than just gripping it by reflex.

fm_004_1.laymanDescription: The baby looks at their own hands and tries to reach for things with both hands.
fm_004_1.howToTest: Watch if your baby spends time looking at their hands. Also, hold a toy in front of them and see if they reach for it with both hands simultaneously.

fm_005_1.laymanDescription: The baby can pick up and hold objects using their whole palm.
fm_005_1.howToTest: Offer your baby a small, safe object like a block. They should be able to wrap their whole hand around it to pick it up.
fm_005_1.redFlagMessage: Unable to hold rattle at 5 months

fm_006_1.laymanDescription: The baby reaches for things with one hand and can move a toy from one hand to the other.
fm_006_1.howToTest: Give your baby a toy. See if they reach for it with one hand and then move it to the other hand to explore it.

fm_009_1.laymanDescription: The baby can pick up small things using their thumb and index finger.
fm_009_1.howToTest: Place a small, safe piece of food (like a soft pea) in front of your baby. See if they use their thumb and finger like a 'pincher' to pick it up.

fm_012_1.laymanDescription: The baby can purposefully release or throw objects.
fm_012_1.howToTest: Observe if your baby intentionally drops or throws toys from their high chair or while playing.

fm_015_1.milestone: Picks up and drinks from cup; scribbles spontaneously; tower of 2 cubes
fm_015_1.laymanDescription: The baby can drink from a cup, scribble with a crayon, and stack two blocks.
fm_015_1.howToTest: Give your baby a crayon and paper to see if they scribble. Also, see if they can stack two small blocks on top of each other.

fm_018_1.milestone: Manages spoon well; tower of 3-4 cubes; turns 2-3 pages
fm_018_1.laymanDescription: The baby can feed themselves with a spoon and stack 3-4 blocks.
fm_018_1.howToTest: Observe your baby during mealtime to see if they can use a spoon. Also, encourage them to stack 3 or 4 blocks in a tower.
fm_018_1.redFlagMessage: Unable to remove socks/gloves at 20 months

fm_024_1.milestone: Removes clothes without buttons; imitates horizontal line and circle; tower of 5-6 cubes; turns page singly
fm_024_1.laymanDescription: The child can take off simple clothes and stack 5-6 blocks.
fm_024_1.howToTest: See if your child can pull off their socks or an unbuttoned shirt. Also, watch them draw or stack blocks to see their progress.

fm_030_1.milestone: Imitates vertical line; tower of 8 cubes
fm_030_1.laymanDescription: The child can draw a straight line up-and-down and stack 8 blocks.
fm_030_1.howToTest: Draw a vertical line and ask your child to do the same. See if they can build a tall tower with 8 blocks.

fm_036_1.milestone: Puts on shoes without lace; unbuttons; imitates cross; copies circle; tower of 9-10 cubes; imitates bridge
fm_036_1.laymanDescription: The child can put on slip-on shoes, unbutton clothes, and draw a circle.
fm_036_1.howToTest: See if your child can help with dressing, specifically with buttons and slip-on shoes. Ask them to draw a circle like yours.
fm_036_1.redFlagMessage: Cannot work simple toys at 36 months

fm_048_1.milestone: Copies cross; draw-a-man 4 parts; incomplete man 3 parts; buttons
fm_048_1.laymanDescription: The child can draw a cross (+), buttons clothes, and draws a simple person.
fm_048_1.howToTest: Ask your child to draw a cross and a person. Check if they can handle small buttons on their clothing.

fm_054_1.milestone: Copies square; copies gate; draw-a-man 6 parts
fm_054_1.laymanDescription: The child can draw a square and a more detailed person.
fm_054_1.howToTest: Ask your child to draw a square and a person with at least 6 distinct parts (head, body, arms, legs).

fm_060_1.milestone: Does not draw picture at 60 months
fm_060_1.laymanDescription: The child should be able to draw recognizable pictures now.
fm_060_1.howToTest: Ask your child to draw a picture of their choice. They should be able to create simple, recognizable drawings.
fm_060_1.redFlagMessage: Does not draw picture at 60 months

sa_002_1.laymanDescription: Your baby starts to smile back at you when you smile or talk to them.
sa_002_1.howToTest: Make eye contact, smile, and talk gently to your baby. Look for a responsive smile.

sa_003_1.laymanDescription: The baby recognizes their primary caregiver (usually the mother) and may react with excitement.
sa_003_1.howToTest: Observe if your baby calms down or gets excited when they see or hear you approaching.

sa_005_1.laymanDescription: The baby enjoys looking at themselves in a mirror and often smiles at the 'other baby'.
sa_005_1.howToTest: Hold your baby in front of a safe mirror and see if they look at their reflection and smile.

sa_006_1.milestone: Stretches arms to be held; stranger anxiety
sa_006_1.laymanDescription: The baby reaches out to be picked up and may be wary of people they don't know.
sa_006_1.howToTest: Reach toward your baby to pick them up; they should stretch their arms back. Also, notice if they act shy or cry around strangers.

sa_007_1.laymanDescription: The baby turns their head or looks at you when you call their name.
sa_007_1.howToTest: While your baby is looking away, call their name in a normal voice and see if they turn to look at you.

sa_009_1.milestone: Waves bye-bye; pulls clothes to attract attention; repeats performance laughed at; responds to 'where is daddy?'
sa_009_1.laymanDescription: The baby can wave, tries to get your attention, and understands simple questions.
sa_009_1.howToTest: Wave 'bye-bye' and see if they imitate. Ask 'Where is [Daddy/Mommy]?' and see if they look for that person.

sa_012_1.laymanDescription: The baby understands and participates in simple games like peek-a-boo.
sa_012_1.howToTest: Cover your face or the baby's face with a cloth and play peek-a-boo. See if they try to uncover your face or play along.

sa_015_1.laymanDescription: The child shows they are uncomfortable when their diaper is wet or soiled.
sa_015_1.howToTest: Observe if your child pulls at their diaper or comes to you when it needs changing.

sa_018_1.milestone: Domestic mimicry; points to 2-3 body parts; dry by day
sa_018_1.laymanDescription: The child copies simple chores and can point to body parts like 'nose' or 'eyes'.
sa_018_1.howToTest: Pretend to sweep or dust and see if they 'help'. Ask 'Where is your nose?' and see if they point correctly.

sa_024_1.laymanDescription: The child plays near other children but mostly plays on their own.
sa_024_1.howToTest: Watch your child in a setting with other toddlers. They should be content playing alongside others, even if they aren't playing 'together' yet.

sa_030_1.laymanDescription: The child can tell you their name and if they are a boy or a girl.
sa_030_1.howToTest: Ask your child 'What is your name?' and 'Are you a boy or a girl?'.

sa_036_1.laymanDescription: The child starts to play with other children and shares their toys.
sa_036_1.howToTest: Observe your child playing with a peer. See if they take turns or share a toy with minimal prompting.

sa_048_1.laymanDescription: The child can go to the bathroom and handle most of the process independently.
sa_048_1.howToTest: See if your child can tell you when they need to go and if they can manage the basics of using the toilet on their own.
sa_048_1.redFlagMessage: Does not respond to peers at 48 months

sa_060_1.milestone: Distinguishes morning from noon; compares 2 weights
sa_060_1.laymanDescription: The child understands basic time concepts and can tell which of two objects is heavier.
sa_060_1.howToTest: Ask your child if it's morning or afternoon. Give them two objects of different weights and ask which one is heavier.
sa_060_1.redFlagMessage: Unusually withdrawn and inactive at 60 months

lg_001_1.laymanDescription: The baby stops moving or quietens down when they hear a clear sound like a bell.
lg_001_1.howToTest: Gently ring a small bell or make a clear sound near your baby (but out of sight) and see if they react by becoming still or quiet.

lg_002_1.milestone: Smiles and vocalises when talked to
lg_002_1.laymanDescription: The baby makes little cooing sounds and smiles when you have a 'conversation' with them.
lg_002_1.howToTest: Talk to your baby while looking at them. They should respond with smiles and small vocal sounds.

lg_003_1.laymanDescription: The baby makes happy squealing sounds and starts to make vowel-like sounds.
lg_003_1.howToTest: Engage in playful interaction with your baby and listen for happy squeals or 'aah' and 'ooh' sounds.

lg_004_1.laymanDescription: The baby starts making more complex sounds that sound like 'aah-goo'.
lg_004_1.howToTest: Listen to your baby's babbles; you should hear them combining vowels with 'g' or 'k' sounds.

lg_005_1.laymanDescription: The baby makes 'raspberry' sounds by blowing through their lips.
lg_005_1.howToTest: Your baby might do this on their own, or you can try blowing a raspberry on their tummy to see if they imitate the sound.

lg_006_1.laymanDescription: The baby starts saying single syllables like 'ba', 'da', or 'ma'.
lg_006_1.howToTest: Listen for your baby repeating single sounds clearly while they are playing or 'talking' to you.

lg_008_1.laymanDescription: The baby starts combining sounds into two syllables, like 'ba-ba' or 'da-da', though they don't mean 'Daddy' yet.
lg_008_1.howToTest: Listen for your baby repeating the same syllable twice in a row.

lg_012_1.laymanDescription: The baby says a few simple words and actually knows what they mean (like 'Mama' for mother).
lg_012_1.howToTest: Listen for your baby using specific words to refer to people or objects correctly.
lg_012_1.redFlagMessage: No babbling / vocal imitation at 12 months

lg_015_1.laymanDescription: The baby speaks in long 'sentences' that sound like real speech but are mostly made-up words.
lg_015_1.howToTest: Observe if your baby has long 'conversations' with themselves or their toys using varied tones and sounds.

lg_018_1.laymanDescription: The child has a small vocabulary of about 10 words that they use correctly.
lg_018_1.howToTest: Count how many different words your child uses consistently to name things or request help.

lg_024_1.laymanDescription: The child knows and uses about 50 different words.
lg_024_1.howToTest: By this age, your child should have a wide range of words for familiar objects, people, and actions.
lg_024_1.redFlagMessage: Single word vocab <= 10 words at 24 months

lg_030_1.laymanDescription: The child starts putting two words together, like 'more milk' or 'doggy bark'.
lg_030_1.howToTest: Listen for your child combining two words to express a more complex thought.

lg_036_1.milestone: 250 words; questions; uses pronouns; nursery rhymes
lg_036_1.laymanDescription: The child has a large vocabulary, asks lots of 'why' questions, and knows some rhymes.
lg_036_1.howToTest: Is your child asking questions and using words like 'I', 'me', and 'you'? Can they sing a bit of a simple nursery rhyme?
lg_036_1.redFlagMessage: < 200 words; no telegraphic sentence at 36 months

lg_042_1.laymanDescription: The child can say complete, simple sentences to tell you what they are thinking.
lg_042_1.howToTest: Listen for your child using 3 or 4 words together to form a proper sentence.
lg_042_1.redFlagMessage: < 600 words; no simple sentences at 42 months

lg_048_1.milestone: Speaks in sentences of 5-6 words; tells long stories
lg_048_1.laymanDescription: The child can tell you about their day or a story using longer sentences.
lg_048_1.howToTest: Ask your child to tell you about something that happened earlier. They should be able to explain it with several sentences.

lg_060_1.milestone: Sentences > 5 words; uses future tense; says name and address
lg_060_1.laymanDescription: The child speaks very clearly, knows their own address, and can talk about things that will happen in the future.
lg_060_1.howToTest: Ask your child 'What are we doing tomorrow?'. They should use future-tense words like 'will' or 'going to'.

hv_000_1.milestone: Horizontal tracking of mother's face to 180 degrees; follows dangling ring to 45 degrees
hv_000_1.laymanDescription: The newborn can follow a face or a bright object moving slowly from side to side.
hv_000_1.howToTest: Hold your face about 10-12 inches from your baby and move slowly from one side to the other. See if their eyes follow you.

hv_001_1.laymanDescription: The baby starts to follow objects moving up and down.
hv_001_1.howToTest: Hold a colorful toy in front of your baby and move it slowly up and down. See if they track it with their eyes.

hv_003_1.milestone: Follows to 180 degrees; looks at objects in midline; turns head to sound source
hv_003_1.laymanDescription: The baby can follow an object in a full semi-circle and turns their head toward sounds.
hv_003_1.howToTest: Move a toy in a wide arc from one side of the baby to the other. Also, make a sound (like a rattle) to the side and see if they turn their head.

hv_004_1.milestone: Turns head and eyes toward direction of sound
hv_004_1.laymanDescription: The baby consistently looks toward where a sound is coming from.
hv_004_1.howToTest: Call your baby or shake a toy from across the room. They should turn their head and eyes to find the source of the sound.

hv_005_1.laymanDescription: The baby can focus both eyes together to see objects clearly at different distances.
hv_005_1.howToTest: Watch your baby as they look at a toy you move closer to and further from their face. Their eyes should move together smoothly.

hv_006_1.milestone: Turns head sidewards and below toward sound
hv_006_1.laymanDescription: The baby can find sounds coming from below them by turning their head.
hv_006_1.howToTest: While your baby is sitting or being held, make a sound below their eye level. They should look down toward the sound.

hv_007_1.milestone: Adjusts position to see object; turns head sidewards and upward; starts imitating sounds
hv_007_1.laymanDescription: The baby moves their body to see something better and starts to copy noises you make.
hv_007_1.howToTest: Place an interesting toy just out of direct view. See if they lean or turn to see it. Try making a simple sound (like 'ba-ba') and see if they try to repeat it.

hv_008_1.laymanDescription: The baby is beginning to understand that their name refers to them.
hv_008_1.howToTest: Call your baby's name while they are engaged with a toy. They should stop and look at you.

hv_010_1.milestone: Turns head diagonally and directly toward sound source
hv_010_1.laymanDescription: The baby can quickly and accurately find where a sound is coming from, even if it's not directly to their side.
hv_010_1.howToTest: Make a soft sound from a corner of the room while the baby is playing. They should look directly at the source almost immediately.

hv_012_1.milestone: Ability to localize sound as good as adult; begins to appreciate forms and colours
hv_012_1.laymanDescription: The child can find sounds perfectly and shows interest in different shapes and bright colors.
hv_012_1.howToTest: Offer your child toys of different colors and shapes. They should show clear preferences and be able to find hidden sound-making toys easily.

hv_030_1.laymanDescription: The child's vision is now as sharp as an adult's.
hv_030_1.howToTest: Observe if your child can see small details in picture books or spot small objects on the floor from a distance.
